sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The state changed:
- The dark gray square at (39-43, 47-51) turned white (id=38, color=W, pixels=12) with orange center at (41, 49) - activated!
- The dark gray diamond at (35, 45) is now dark gray again (id=37, color=G, pixels=8) - it went back to dark gray!
So the pattern is:
1. Click the purple square center -> it moves down, creates new dark gray shapes
2. Click the dark gray diamond center -> it turns white (activated)
3. Click the dark gray square center -> it turns white (activated), but the previous one goes back to dark gray
This is like a "moving activation" pattern. The white activation moves down the chain.
Now I need to figure out what's next. The orange diamond outline at (48-54, 37-43) is still there. Maybe I need to click that.
But wait, looking at the orange chain pattern from earlier:
1. Click orange diamond at (25, 46) -> moves down
2. Click dark gray diamond at (35, 45) -> activates (white)
3. Click purple square at (41, 49) -> activates (white), previous goes back to dark gray
4. Click ring at (48, 54) -> activates (white), previous goes back to dark gray
For the purple chain:
1. Click purple square at (25, 46) -> moves down
2. Click dark gray diamond at (35, 45) -> activates (white)
3. Click dark gray square at (41, 49) -> activates (white), previous goes back to dark gray
4. Click ??? -> next target
The orange diamond outline at (48-54, 37-43) is the next target. Let me click its center at (51, 40).
